title: Chapter Thirty-Three: The Fifth Estate
content: **r/TrueCrime • Posted by u/Redacted_Leak • 6h • 🔒 Locked • 💬 14,827**
## [LEAK] Full preliminary testimony of Margaret Grose — Bly Manor proceedings, Crown v. Wingrave
**CW: sexual exploitation, child abuse, death by negligence, suicide**

I'm not going to stylize this. I'm not going to narrate. I'm going to give you what I have, which is the full preliminary testimony of Margaret Grose, given under caution to the CPS on the 9th of June, and which someone with access decided the public needed to see.
I have verified this document with two independent sources within the court system. I will not say how I obtained it. I will not answer DMs about provenance. Read it and weep.
The full text follows. I have removed only the names of the surviving children.
*Testimony begins. CW applies throughout.*

*MRS. GROSE: I knew what he was. I knew from the first week. Peter told me — he told me the house was a system. He said the women were inputs. I didn't understand. I thought he meant — I thought it was a metaphor. He said, "Margaret, the trick is that they choose to stay. If they choose, it isn't cruelty. It's a game."*

*MRS. GROSE: He had rules. Written rules. He showed me the notebook. Green cover, like a school exercise book. Rule one: the governess must always believe she is the first to notice. Rule two: the children must never break character. Rule three: when she asks if the house is haunted, the answer must always be "I don't know what you mean."*
*MRS. GROSE: The first one was in 2003. I don't remember her name properly. Eastern European. Peter called her "the prototype." She left after three weeks. She was the lucky one.*
*MRS. GROSE: [REDACTED — surviving governess, 2007] broke within two months. She signed the NDA. Peter said she was "revenue neutral."*

*MRS. GROSE: Miss Jessel was the fourth. She was different. She fought. She documented everything. She wrote it down — the voices, the apparitions, the way the children changed when you asked the wrong question. She had a notebook of her own.*
*MRS. GROSE: He found the notebook. He read it to the children like a bedtime story.*

*MRS. GROSE: She took her own life on the 14th of October. I was in the room below. I heard the — I heard. I went upstairs. The boy was filming. He was twelve years old and he was filming, and he looked at me and said, "Peter says this is the final level."*

*MRS. GROSE: Henry Wingrave knew. He funded the property, the legal retainers, the NDAs. He told me once that Peter was "the most innovative mind he'd ever employed." He said it at a dinner party. People laughed. It wasn't a joke.*

*MRS. GROSE: I stayed because I was afraid. I stayed because I thought if I left, there would be no one left to — I told myself I was protecting the children. I was protecting myself. I know that now. I want the court to know that I know.*
*Testimony ends.*

This is not a haunted house story. This is a purpose-built apparatus for the destruction of women, and it operated continuously from 2003 until at minimum last autumn.

She used the same mechanisms. Audience participation. Incremental reveals. Emotional manipulation. We were her eyes and ears and she orchestrated every beat. Are we viewers? Or are we weapons?

She's not wrong to be angry. But let's not pretend she was purely selfless either.

**r/BlyManorGame • Posted by u/ModTeam • 8h • 📌 PINNED**
## Weekly Discussion Thread — We've Hit 200K

It's been a wild ride. When we started this sub it was 400 people trying to solve ARG clues from a TikTok account. Now we're the fastest-growing community on the platform and the actual testimony is public and things are getting — we need to have a conversation.
**New rules, effective immediately:**

1. No doxxing. Not Wingrave, not Grose, not the children, NOT Maeve. We don't dox. Period.
2. No "how to play" threads. We are NOT going to be a place where people share instructions for recreating the Bly Manor methodology. If you post a "rules of the Game" thread, you will be banned and reported.
3. No merchandise links. Yeah, we see you. Stop it.
That is all. Be decent to each other. This isn't entertainment anymore. It never was.

> **u/ghost_protocol_99** • 7h
>
> Too late for rule 3, mods. There's literally a Bly Manor RPG on Roblox. My nephew showed me. You walk through a manor house, you find "clues," the children appear and ask you questions, and when you answer wrong they — look, it's not graphic, but the structure is IDENTICAL. Someone built Peter's Game on Roblox. For children. To play. FOR FUN.

> **u/EtsyWatch_Bot** • 6h

> Current Etsy results for "Peter's Game": 47 listings. T-shirts, stickers, enamel pins of the green notebook, one person selling a "Bly Manor Escape Room Kit" for £85. The internet has done what the internet does. It ate the horror and shat out merchandise.

> **u/medium_rare_truth** • 5h
>
> The Roblox thing is what breaks me. Not because it exists — the internet has always been a machine for turning suffering into content. It breaks me because Peter Quint would have LOVED it. He designed the Game to be self-replicating. He designed it to need an audience. And here we are. The audience is making more games. The Game doesn't need Peter anymore. It never did. He just pointed at something the internet was always going to build on its own.

> **u/hollow_corpus** • 5h
>
> This is what I can't stop thinking about. Take away Peter, take away the manor, take away the money. You'd still get this. You'd still get the subreddit, the ARG, the YouTube explainers, the Roblox clone, the Etsy merch. The architecture of the internet IS the Game. Engagement loops. Escalation mechanics. Revenue from attention. Peter didn't invent a new kind of cruelty. He just ported an old one to a new platform. The platform was waiting for him.
**r/TrueCrime • Posted by u/courtwatcher_UK • 4h • 🔒 Locked**
## Meta: The scale of what's happening

I've been a true crime moderator for nine years. I have never seen anything like this. Not the McCanns, not the Salisbury poisonings, not the plebgate tapes. This is the largest public reaction to a criminal proceeding in British internet history.
By the numbers:

- #BlyManor: 2.3M tweets in 12 hours
- r/BlyManorGame: 203K members (up from 11K two weeks ago)
- YouTube: 8,400+ Bly Manor videos uploaded in the last 24 hours
- TikTok: #BlyManorGame has 480M cumulative views
- Roblox: at least 3 Bly Manor games identified and growing
- Etsy: 47+ listings for Peter's Game merchandise
- CPS phone lines: overloaded, official statement delayed
- Henry Wingrave's Wikipedia page: edited 1,200+ times since the leak, now semi-protected
- Mrs. Grose's relative (identified by the Daily Mail — yes, they doxxed her family): has disabled all social media
The dam has broken. This is no longer a story. It's an event.

> **u/LurkerSince2016** • 3h
>
> "It's no longer a story. It's an event." And events get turned into content. Which gets turned into engagement. Which gets turned into revenue. Which incentivizes more content. The Game's operating system isn't Peter's notebook. It's the attention economy. We're all running Peter's software and we don't even know it.

**[TIKTOK — @Maeve_Bly — Final Post]**

*Video: 1 minute 14 seconds. Maeve, face to camera. No filter. Plain white wall behind her. Her eyes are red. She's been crying, or she's been up all night, or both. Her voice is steady but thin, like a wire at breaking tension.*
**MAEVE:**
I started this because I wanted to find my father.
I found him.
I also found six women he destroyed, two children he broke, and a house that eats people.
I'm going to court next week. I'm going to testify. After that, I'm deleting this account.
The story doesn't belong to me anymore. It belongs to the women who didn't survive it.
Don't play the Game.
Don't sell the Game.
Don't BE the Game.
*She holds the camera for three more seconds. Her jaw tightens. She reaches forward and the video ends.*

*No answer from @Maeve_Bly.*
*The account no longer exists.*

*But the subreddit grows. The Roblox games multiply. The Etsy listings refresh. The algorithm, fed on six months of meticulous reveals and a dead woman's testimony, does what algorithms do: it finds more people who want to look, and it shows them where to look, and it counts their looking as engagement, and it serves them more.*
*Maeve Quint played the Game to end it. She exposed the architecture, named the architect, pulled the house down around her own head and walked away from the rubble.*

*But a game that can be ended by one player can be restarted by another. A game that lives in an audience doesn't die when the performer leaves the stage. A game whose rules are written in attention and escalation and the human hunger to witness suffering at a safe distance — that game doesn't need Peter Quint. It doesn't need Bly Manor. It doesn't even need Maeve.*
*It needs an audience.*
*It has one.*
*The screen turns.*
